summary: High‑energy Madison show with a solid soundboard capture (minor cut) and standout Weather Report Suite and Bobby‑led Saturday Night finale.
review: The 1973‑10‑25 Madison concert is hailed as one of the band’s all‑time greats, featuring an electrifying Weather Report Suite and a vocal flourish from Bob on Saturday Night. The soundboard source is generally clean, though reviewers note a brief cut in the JED segment. Overall the performance and recording are praised as spectacular.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews rate the show highly (4‑5 stars) and praise both the musical flow and the quality of the soundboard recording, with only a minor note about a cut.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced; one reviewer mentions a small cut in the JED segment but no major mix issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as exploratory and tightly sequenced, with the Weather Report Suite highlighted as a big local draw and Bobby's vocal flourishes capping the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Reviewers rave about Jerry’s string‑bending toward the end of the JED tune, calling his playing insane and out‑of‑this‑world.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocal flourishes on Saturday Night are praised as a strong finish to the show.
